products.name: Xico Clean
products.tagline: Make your Mac fast and clean again

capabilities.title: One set of capabilities, for products and projects
capabilities.subtitle: Each of these runs in our own products first, then goes into client work.

items.title: AI Application Development
items.desc: LLM applications, knowledge bases, and automated workflows — from solution design through development to evaluation. Xico Clean's smart scanning and image recognition test this capability daily.

items.title: Native Apple Platforms
items.desc: macOS and iOS development in Swift 6 + SwiftUI: deep system integration, performance work, notarized distribution. We have done every step of this for our own products.

items.title: Web & Mobile
items.desc: Multi-platform development with Next.js, iOS, and Android, covering admin consoles, payments, and internationalization. Machi and this site run on this stack.

items.title: Delivery & Maintenance
items.desc: Deployment, monitoring, data safety, and a steady release rhythm. After launch we stay responsible — no drop-and-run delivery.

work.title: Built by us. Maintained for years.
work.subtitle: Xico Clean and Machi are built and operated by XICO. If you are evaluating us for a project, start by trying them.

items.name: Xico Clean
items.kind: macOS cleaner & optimizer · Flagship desktop app
items.tagline: Make your Mac fast and clean again
items.about: Xico Clean is XICO's flagship desktop app — a premium macOS cleaner and optimizer built to rival CleanMyMac X. Native Swift 6 + SwiftUI, deeply integrated with the system: one-tap Smart Scan, dedicated developer-junk cleanup, Space Lens visualization, duplicate and similar-photo detection, thorough uninstalls, privacy and maintenance. Every deletion is previewable and undoable, and everything runs locally.
items.desc: A native Swift-built Mac cleaner: Smart Scan, developer junk, space visualization, duplicate files and privacy cleanup — all local, previewable and undoable.

items.name: Machi
items.kind: Everyday app for life in Japan
items.tagline: Life in Japan — one app is enough
items.desc: A one-stop platform for students, workers, and newcomers in Japan: city guides, housing, jobs, a second-hand market, and local community.
items.about: Machi is built for everyone living in Japan — students, professionals, and new residents. When you arrive in a new city, the hardest part is decoding local rules and finding trustworthy resources. Machi brings city guides, housing, jobs, second-hand listings, community, and lived experience into one place, shortening the distance between looking something up and getting it done.

items.name: Shangence
items.tagline: Decision support before opening a business
items.desc: Risk assessment, validation budget, and action planning for small founders.
items.about: Shangence helps founders make a more grounded decision before the first major spend. It reviews industry conditions, capital safety, personal capability, acquisition difficulty, and external constraints, then returns a risk score, validation budget, stop-loss line, and 30-day action plan. It does not promise success; it gives the decision better evidence.

items.name: Game development
items.desc: We are bringing our native engineering, graphics, and interaction experience into games. Partnership enquiries are welcome.
items.about: Years of native performance work and graphics experience will shape how we approach games: small teams, high polish. We are still in preparation — follow our updates, or get in touch about partnerships.

approach.title: The rules we build by
approach.subtitle: These principles come from building products daily, and they govern every project we deliver.

principles.title: Define the problem first
principles.desc: Before any code: confirm the goal, the users, and the boundaries. Clear scope is what makes timelines and budgets honest.

principles.title: Details make the quality
principles.desc: Interfaces, copy, error states, loading speed — users feel all of it. We don't let these slide.

principles.title: Data safety is a precondition
principles.desc: What can be processed locally stays local, and client data has explicit permission boundaries. This is not negotiable.

principles.title: Responsible after launch
principles.desc: Our products keep getting updates; client projects get long-term maintenance. We are not a team that disappears after handover.

cta.title: Discuss your project, or try our products first.
cta.subtitle: Planning an AI application or a software project? Write to us with the background and goals. Or download Xico Clean first and see our work for yourself.

about.title: A development company that builds its own products.
about.subtitle: Zhixike Technology (Guangzhou) Co., Ltd. builds its own software products and provides AI and software development services under the XICO AI｜智希可 brand.
story: XICO is an AI and software development company based in Guangzhou. We have two products of our own — Xico Clean, a macOS cleaner and optimizer, and Machi, an everyday app for life in Japan — and we provide AI application and custom software development for clients.

We keep building our own products because they force us to face real users: whether a need is real, whether the experience holds up, whether the system stays maintainable — it all shows up in downloads and reviews. That judgment goes straight into client projects.
Next, we are moving into game development. It is a natural extension of our engineering, graphics, and interaction work, and a direction we are committed to for the long term.

items.title: Product-grade standards
items.desc: Client projects and our own products are built by the same people to the same standard. There is no 'internal' versus 'external' quality.

items.title: Performance and privacy
items.desc: Native first, local first. User data and client data both have clear boundaries.

items.title: Responsible long-term
items.desc: Products keep getting updates; projects get ongoing maintenance. We want every working relationship to be measured in years.

founder.name: Yao Kai · 姚凱
founder.quote: Engineering quality and experience quality are the same thing.
bio: I started out full-stack: native Swift clients, backends, payments, deployment, and operations — all hands-on. XICO's products and project delivery are built on that first-hand experience.
Our own products keep the team sharp. Xico Clean and Machi are used by real people every day, and nothing rough stays hidden for long. Client work benefits from that same pressure.
XICO will keep building products, delivering services, and moving into games. The business will grow; the standard stays the same.

faq.title: Questions we often get
faq.subtitle: If yours isn't here, just write to us.

items.q: What kind of company is XICO?
items.a: An AI and software development company based in Guangzhou. We build our own products — Xico Clean and Machi — and provide AI application and custom software development for clients.

items.q: Do you take custom development projects?
items.a: Yes. AI applications, web, mobile, and desktop are all in scope. Client projects are delivered to the same standard as our own products, with long-term maintenance after launch.

Xico Clean, a native macOS cleaner and optimizer — buy once, fully local, free trial on this site. And Machi, a one-stop app for life in Japan — the web version is live, with mobile apps in development.
Yes — a game development practice is in preparation. Our native engineering and graphics experience will carry into it. Follow our updates, or get in touch about partnerships.
Email us or use the contact form with the project background, goals, and expected timeline. We usually reply within 1–2 business days, and we talk it through before quoting.

items.q: Where can I try your products?
items.a: Xico Clean is a free download on this site with a 15-day full-feature trial. Machi is available at machicity.com.

manifesto.title: Good software is worth real effort.
body: Our products and client projects share one standard: clear structure, solid performance, and long-term maintainability. That is what makes software hold up over time.
AI is changing how software is built and what it can be. We put it to work on real problems — in our own products first, and for clients once it has proven itself.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Zhixike Technology (Guangzhou) Co., Ltd. (“XICO AI”, “we”) respects and protects your privacy. This policy explains what information we collect on xicoai.com, how we use it, and the rights you have.

sections.p: When you contact us through a form, a partnership enquiry or a job application, we collect the information you choose to provide — such as your name, email, company, message, and the résumé file you upload when applying. We also collect basic technical logs (time of visit, pages, device type) to keep the site secure and stable.

We use this information only to respond to your enquiries, process collaboration or job applications, and operate and improve the site. We do not sell your personal information or use it for unrelated purposes.
Information is stored on servers under our control and protected with reasonable technical and organizational measures. We keep it only as long as needed for the purposes above.
We use only essential local storage to remember your theme (light/dark) and language preference. We do not use it for cross-site tracking.
You can contact us at hi@xicoai.com at any time to access, correct or delete your personal information.
We may update this policy from time to time. Material changes will be posted on this page. Questions? Email hi@xicoai.com.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: Welcome to xicoai.com. By accessing or using this site you agree to the following terms. This site is operated by Zhixike Technology (Guangzhou) Co., Ltd. (“XICO AI”).

sections.p: This site presents XICO AI's products, capabilities and company information, and provides ways to get in touch.

Unless otherwise stated, the text, design, marks and content of this site belong to XICO AI and may not be copied or used commercially without permission. Third-party brand marks belong to their respective owners.
Content on this site is provided “as is”. We aim to keep it accurate but make no warranty as to its completeness or timeliness.
To the extent permitted by law, XICO AI is not liable for any indirect or incidental loss arising from use of this site.
These terms are governed by the laws of the People's Republic of China.
For any questions about these terms, contact hi@xicoai.com.

hero.eyebrow: Native macOS app · Built for Apple silicon
hero.subtitle: Xico Clean finds system junk, developer caches, large files, and duplicates in a single tap, then safely frees up disk space — with a DaisyDisk-class Space Lens and iStat-class menu-bar monitoring included. Built in native Swift and fully local: preview before you delete, undo after.

features.title: One app to look after your entire Mac
features.subtitle: From system junk to developer caches, from space visualization to privacy cleanup — Xico Clean brings everything Mac maintenance needs into one polished native app.

items.name: Smart Scan
items.desc: System junk, Trash, threats, duplicates, similar photos, and large files — six scans at once, each appearing as it finishes, honestly separating space you reclaim now from space macOS manages. One safe click to clean.

items.name: System Junk
items.desc: User and system caches, logs, language files, old update packages, and app caches — cleared with precision.

items.name: Developer Junk
items.desc: Xcode DerivedData, simulators, node_modules, Homebrew / npm / pip caches and dangling Docker images.

items.name: Space Lens
items.desc: A DaisyDisk-class sunburst chart: your whole disk scanned in about 90 seconds, physical footprint precisely reconciled, drill-down to any file, and safe click-to-delete.

items.name: Large & Old Files
items.desc: Filter by size and last-accessed date to quickly surface the big files worth clearing.

items.name: Duplicates
items.desc: Content-level deduplication that recognizes APFS clones to avoid false positives, and smartly keeps one copy.

items.name: Similar Photos
items.desc: On-device Vision clustering finds duplicate and near-identical photos, keeping the best shot.

items.name: Uninstaller
items.desc: Remove apps completely — along with their caches, preferences, containers, login items, and receipts.

items.name: Privacy Cleanup
items.desc: Clear history, cookies, and caches across Safari, Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and Arc.

items.name: Optimize & Maintain
items.desc: Manage login items, free up memory, run maintenance scripts, rebuild the index, and flush DNS.

items.name: Menu Bar Monitor
items.desc: Lives in the menu bar with live CPU, memory, and network stats, plus quick cleanup always at hand.

items.name: Malware Removal
items.desc: Scan for known macOS adware and malicious components using a signed definitions library, and remove them on detection.

deepdive.title: Turning tedious cleanup into something that feels good
deepdive.subtitle: A few capabilities you'll remember at a glance — the ones we poured the most care into polishing.

items.title: One tap, and your whole Mac feels new
items.desc: System junk, privacy traces, and performance issues, cleared in a single pass. After the signature scan animation, you get a clear overview card — how much you can reclaim and what we found. Only 100% safe items are checked by default, and you can review every one.
points: Combined scan: system junk, privacy, and performance
Only 100% safe items checked by default
A reclaimable-space overview when it's done

items.title: A DaisyDisk-class engine — every gigabyte in sight
items.desc: Under the hood it uses the same system bulk-scanning API as DaisyDisk, mapping your entire disk into a sunburst chart in about 90 seconds. It counts precise physical footprint — hard links, system-volume clones, and sparse files all add up correctly, with even “hidden space” fully reconciled. Drill down level by level to any single file, then click to preview, collect, and delete safely.
points: System-level bulk scanning · full disk in about 90 seconds
Physical footprint reconciled · matches disk usage exactly
Unlimited drill-down · click to delete · always undoable

items.title: Reclaim tens of gigabytes — made for developers
items.desc: Xcode's DerivedData, DeviceSupport, Archives, and old simulators, plus node_modules, Homebrew / npm / pip caches, and dangling Docker images — the developer junk that quietly eats tens of gigabytes, all found in one pass.
points: Dangling Docker images and old simulators

items.title: Duplicate files and near-identical photos — keep only the best
items.desc: Content-level hashing finds duplicates precisely and recognizes APFS clones to avoid false positives, while similar-photo detection uses on-device Vision clustering to help you keep the best shot from a burst. Always local, never uploaded.

safety.title: The biggest fear in cleaning is deleting the wrong thing — so we build peace of mind into every step
safety.subtitle: The biggest trust risk in any cleaner is deleting the wrong thing. So Xico Clean puts three independent gates in front of every deletion — plus red lines, previews, and undo. Every step leaves a way back.

items.title: Always preview, then delete
items.desc: Every item expands to show its path and size — nothing moves until you confirm.

items.title: Undoable
items.desc: Items go to the Trash or a recoverable area first, so you can undo your last cleanup anytime.

items.title: Whitelist & ignore list
items.desc: Permanently protect certain paths, and Xico Clean will never touch them again.

items.title: Red lines, never crossed
items.desc: Critical system paths, SSH keys and cloud credentials (like ~/.ssh and ~/.aws), the keychain — the rule library refuses them outright, no exceptions.

items.title: Three independent checks
items.desc: A pre-check in the UI → a second pass by the safety engine → a final verification by the system-level helper right before deletion — all three sharing one rule library.

items.title: Transparent by design
items.desc: Every category explains what it is and what happens if you delete it — never a black box.

items.title: Xico Safety Library
items.desc: A continuously updated set of cleanup rules — every removable item explains why it’s safe to delete, and false positives can be reported and ignored locally in one click. All three checkpoints share the same rules.

items.title: Trash Sentinel
items.desc: Drag an app to the Trash and Xico detects its leftover caches, preferences, and launch items — grouped by the original app, alerts only, never deleting anything automatically.

privacy.title: Your files stay on your Mac — always
privacy.subtitle: Cleaning is deeply personal. All scanning and analysis in Xico Clean happen locally — we neither want to, nor are able to, see your files.
points: All scanning and analysis run locally
No file contents are ever uploaded
Telemetry is anonymous and can be turned off in one tap
Similar-photo detection is pure on-device Vision

performance.title: Built in native Swift — so fast you'll barely notice it
performance.subtitle: We flatly refuse to use Electron. Xico Clean is thoroughly native and built for Apple silicon — fast to scan, light on resources, smooth in motion.

items.label: smooth even with huge numbers of files
never nags or slows down your Mac

items.a: macOS 14 Sonoma and later, as a universal binary for both Apple silicon and Intel — one installer for all.

items.q: Is it safe? Could it delete the wrong thing?
items.a: Every deletion is previewable and undoable, a built-in protection list guards critical system files, and only 100% safe items are checked by default.
No. All scanning and analysis happen locally, no file contents are ever uploaded, and telemetry can be turned off in one tap.
No. Xico Clean is a one-time purchase with free updates for the long haul, plus a 15-day full-feature trial.

items.q: Will it be on the App Store?
items.a: It's distributed through Apple-notarized Developer ID, so its features aren't limited by the sandbox — and it's just as safe to install.
Yes. Download it free from this site — 15-day full-feature trial, no sign-up. After purchase, enter your activation key to unlock everything.

items.q: How is Xico Clean different from CleanMyMac?
items.a: Xico Clean is a native Swift app you buy once — a single payment, free updates for the long haul, and no subscription. Beyond deep cleaning and complete uninstalls, it adds a live menu-bar monitor, hardware and battery health, a real 10 GB disk speed benchmark, and Space Lens disk visualization — folding what used to take several separate apps into one. Everything runs locally, and your files are never uploaded.

items.q: How is Space Lens different from DaisyDisk?
items.a: Under the hood it uses the same system bulk-scanning API, so speed and accuracy are in the same class: counted by physical footprint, hard links counted once, even “hidden space” fully reconciled. On top of that, you can drill down to any file and delete it safely right there (undoable, of course) — and it's just one of a dozen-plus tools in Xico Clean, so there's no separate disk visualizer to buy.

items.q: What is System Data on Mac and how do I clear it?
items.a: System Data (once called “Other”) is macOS caches, logs, local snapshots and app support files. Much of it is safe to remove. Xico Clean’s Smart Scan finds the reclaimable part and honestly separates what you free now from space macOS manages, so you clear System Data without guesswork — preview first, undo anytime.

items.q: Why is my Mac slow, and how do I speed it up?
items.a: A nearly full disk, bloated caches, heavy login items and runaway apps all slow a Mac down. Xico Clean frees disk space, clears safe junk, and its menu-bar monitor shows what’s using CPU, memory and disk in real time — so you can spot and fix the cause, not just the symptom.

items.q: How do I clean my Mac and free up disk space?
items.a: Open Xico Clean and tap Smart Scan — within seconds it surfaces system junk, developer caches, large files, and duplicates. Then open Space Lens to see your whole disk as an interactive map and pinpoint the folders eating the most room. A safe cleanup can reclaim anywhere from a few to dozens of gigabytes.

items.q: Is there a free Mac cleaner?
items.a: Xico Clean includes a 15-day full-feature free trial — no signup needed to download and put the complete cleanup and optimization toolkit to work. After the trial, a single one-time purchase keeps it yours for good. It's not a subscription.

footer.tagline: Xico Clean — a native cleaner and optimizer for Mac. Native, safe, and fully local.

download.title: Give your Mac a fresh start
download.subtitle: Free download, 15-day full trial. Native Apple silicon, Apple-notarized, ready the moment you open it.
download.trust: Notarized — Gatekeeper approves it directly. Just drag it into Applications after downloading.

lens.title: Your whole disk, at a glance
lens.desc: The same system-level bulk-scanning engine as DaisyDisk analyzes your whole disk in about 90 seconds. It counts what files really occupy on disk — hard links and system-volume clones are never counted twice, so the center figure matches your used space exactly, with even “hidden space” like system snapshots fully accounted for. The sunburst chart drills down without limit: click a folder to step inside, click a file to select it, then right-click or drag it into the Collector to delete safely.
points: Same scan engine as DaisyDisk · full disk in about 90 seconds
Physical footprint, fully reconciled · hidden space included
Drill down to any file · select and delete safely
lens.caption: Space Lens · sunburst chart

speed.title: Reads, writes, random IOPS, and video fit — more pro than Blackmagic
speed.desc: Aligned buffers and two concurrent streams read the medium directly — measured sequential speeds beat Blackmagic. Add random 4K IOPS with p50/p99 latency (the truth behind how responsive your Mac feels) and a ProRes / BRAW video-workflow fit table. Fully random, incompressible data measures sustained real performance, not a cache mirage.
points: Aligned buffers + two streams — measured to beat Blackmagic
F_NOCACHE reads the medium directly · fully random, incompressible data
speed.caption: Disk Speed Test · sequential benchmark

allinone.title: Four pro tools' capabilities. One purchase.
allinone.desc: Deep cleaning, live monitoring, hardware health, disk tools — capabilities that used to take four separately-paid apps, all covered by one Xico Clean.
allinone.footNote: Each sold separately, some by subscription; Xico Clean covers it all with one purchase.

showcase.title: A full toolkit, one tap each
showcase.lede: Junk cleanup, complete uninstall, duplicate finder, optimization — each one refined, all in one app.
